test.command: python3 tests/val_m2_fused_gemv_ar.py (local syntax check; full test requires dev server)
test.observation: Validation test syntax verified. Full GPU tests require running on dev server.

typecheck.command: Python syntax: python3 -m py_compile; C syntax: gcc -fsyntax-only src/runtime/c_dispatch.c
typecheck.observation: Python files compile without errors. C dispatch compiles (other C files have x86 intrinsics, expected for ARM macOS).

lint.command: No lint tool configured; manual code review performed
lint.observation: Code follows project conventions. Kernel is well-documented with clear phase structure.

blockingIssues.description: Fused GEMV+AR+RMSNorm kernel causes 71% throughput regression (45 tok/s -> 15 tok/s) even after input buffer fix. Kernel is disabled by default pending investigation. Root cause unknown.

m2-fused-gemv-kernel.description: Fused INT4 GEMV + P2P Allreduce + RMSNorm kernel implementation
positiveObservations: Well-documented kernel with clear phase structure (GEMV, P2P Allreduce, RMSNorm)
Supports both TP=2 and TP=4 configurations
Proper cooperative reduction via DPP + LDS

m2-c-dispatch-fused.description: C dispatch integration for fused kernel
positiveObservations: Proper extension of CAllreduceSpec struct with fused kernel fields (use_gemv_fused, gemv_fused_tp4_fn, ffn_gate_ptrs, etc.)
dispatch_allreduce() correctly routes to fused kernel when enabled
Correctly skips ffn_down and next attn_rmsnorm when fused kernel is used
Input buffer fix applied: uses ffn_gate_ptrs instead of hidden_ptrs[0]

issues.description: Fused kernel still causes regression after input buffer fix. Kernel disabled by default (commit 39ffd0d).
Root cause of regression not fully identified. Input buffer was wrong, but fix did not resolve the throughput issue.
Consider profiling kernel execution to identify bottleneck: memory bandwidth, synchronization overhead, or computation errors.

timeline: Fix attempt (0dbe5e4): Fixed input buffer (hidden_ptrs[0] -> ffn_gate_ptrs)
Final state (39ffd0d): Still causes regression, disabled pending investigation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: When implementing kernel fusion, always validate with A/B benchmark comparison before merging. Fused kernels must demonstrate no regression AND numerical correctness.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Fused GEMV+AR+RMSNorm kernel caused 71% regression. Even after fixing a known bug (wrong input buffer), the regression persists. This could have been caught earlier with mandatory benchmark validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: For GPU kernel optimization work, always validate on the target hardware (dev server) before marking complete. Local compilation/syntax checks are insufficient for performance validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fused kernel regression was only discovered when benchmarking on the dev server with actual GPU execution. Local tests passed but the performance issue was hardware-specific.

summary.whatWasBuilt: Fused GEMV+P2P Allreduce+RMSNorm kernel for FFN down projection in TP inference. Infrastructure for kernel fusion is in place with proper C dispatch integration. Input buffer bug was identified and fixed.
summary.currentStatus: Fused kernel is DISABLED by default due to unresolved throughput regression (45 tok/s -> 15 tok/s). Baseline throughput of 44+ tok/s is maintained with the kernel disabled. No new fixes have been added since Round 1.
summary.nextSteps: Investigate root cause of regression in fused kernel. Possible areas: (1) memory bandwidth contention from P2P reads during GEMV, (2) kernel launch overhead not actually reduced, (3) synchronization patterns, (4) numerical issues causing incorrect computation. Consider profiling with ROCm profiler.
